title: proboscidea louisianica subsp. fragrans
intro: Proboscidea louisianica subsp. fragrans, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, is a perennial herb native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Proboscidea louisianica subsp. fragrans include relief from muscle spasms, menstrual cramps, and digestive problems, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various forms of use in traditional medicine and holistic healing practices.
